text:
Now I have the full picture. Let me analyze this step by step.

**Context**: Single event — a geofence exit (severity 3) detected from a guard device with `background: true`. Evidence level E1.

**Prior**: P(real)=0.4 but n=0 — this is a hand-set assumption with zero observations behind it. Weak starting point.

**Precedents**: Zero — genuinely novel for this site/event-type.

**Board Load**: 20 items/10min — well into FLOOD territory (threshold=6). Rule R-004 explicitly says: *"Under flood conditions (>6/10min), suppress E0-E1 to manage operator load."*

This is a guard device geofence exit with a "background" flag — most likely a guard crossing a virtual boundary during routine patrol. Very low security concern. The operator is already drowning at 20 items. Surfaces a routine guard-geofence event would waste their attention.

Suppressing under policy R-004 is the right call.

function.name: make_decision
function.arguments: {"chosen_move": "commit", "action_id": "suppress", "prior_adjustment_log_odds": -1.0, "adjustment_reasons": ["Guard device geofence exit with background flag — likely routine patrol crossing", "Board load at 20/10min (flood) — suppress per policy R-004", "Hand-set prior (n=0) — weak prior, freely adjusted downward"], "novelty_flag": true, "confidence_p_real": 0.15, "what_would_change_my_mind": "If this geofence exit correlates with a concurrent alarm type (e.g., intrusion, duress) or if the guard device shows abnormal movement patterns outside of known patrol routes. A guard check-in would also raise confidence.\""}
